''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature et al., Mainz et al. 1950.  [[Category:New Year]] {{draft categories|[[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:New Year]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[en:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]]}}

[[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. ''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature et al., Mainz et al. 1950.  [[Category:New Year]] {{draft categories|[[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:New Year]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)-qq]] [[en:draft:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)-qq]] [[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]]}}

{{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature et al., Mainz et al. 1950.  [[Category:New Year]] {{draft categories|[[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:New Year]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)-qq]] [[en:draft:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)-qq]] [[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]]}}

{{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The [[:de:Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe]] dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re-Harachte]]''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Re-Harachte|de}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}}''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). ''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' ''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). Publisher of the Academy of Sciences and Literature et al., Mainz et al. 1950.  [[Category:nowiki former opening]] [[Category:New Year]][[Category:nowiki former closing]] {{draft categories|[[Category:History of timekeeping]] [[Category:New Year]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[en:draft:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[de:Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)]] [[fr:user:immanuelle/New Year's Festival (Ancient Egypt)]]}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Ill|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Ill|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Ill|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' {{Infobox hieroglyphs|TITEL=Book of the Day|name=wn:N35:N35-W24:X1-N3:N5-Z1-Z2:N35-h-r:Z7*N5|NAME-TRANSKRIPTION=wenut-net-heru
''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}}''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ). ''wnwt-nt-hrw''
''Book of the Day''|Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig|TITEL=Neujahrsfest|NAME=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-G7-Axt:t*y
Heb mesiu Re-Harachte
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ-w-ḥr w-3ḫ.tj''
''Fest der Geburt von {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}}''|NAME-ERWEITERT=[[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Altes Reich]]|NAME2-ERWEITERT=[[12. Dynastie|12. Dynastie]]|NAME2=W3-ms-M17-w-ra:Z1-C2
Heb mesiu Re
''Ḥb msjw Rˁ''
''Fest der Geburt von [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]]''|NAME3-ERWEITERT='''Festdatum'''|NAME3=T8-M5-F15
''Tpj-rnp.t wpt-rnp.t''
''Erster Tag des Jahres, Öffner des Jahres''}}{{complex}} The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''New Year''' ( '''Heb mesiu Re''' ) was celebrated on the first day of the month {{ill|Wepet-renpet|de}} under the nickname '''Heb-tepi-renpet wepet-renpet''', which in [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|the Old]] and [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdoms was]] also the first month of the {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en}} and the birthday of the deity {{ill|Ra-Horakhty|de|Re-Harachte}} or [[Ra|Re]] . == background == The New Year was fixed in the [[Egyptian calendar|Egyptian administrative calendar]] and had its origins in the Sothis {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt=Lunar Calendar|||de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|en:draft|Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|en:draft}} . The celebrations were held on the fixed calendar date regardless of the [[Flooding of the Nile|Nile flood]] . The ancient Egyptian New Year festival began “on the night of the fifth day of the season {{ill|Heriu-renpet|en||de}} ”. With the first rays of the sun on the first day of Achet I, the new year began shortly thereafter. The "birth of Re" initiated in the old year and was completed in the new year at the time of "the appearance of Re." In this context, Achet I marks the start of the inundation season in the ancient Egyptian calendar, an event crucial for the agricultural rebirth brought by the annual flooding of the Nile. The Egyptians called the second New Year celebration the {{ill|Sothis-Festival|de|Sothis-Fest}}, which was tied to the {{ill|Heliacal rising|lt=Heliacal|de|Heliakisch|en|lt_original=Heliakisch}} rising of [[Sirius]] . The Egyptians therefore usually celebrated the New Year twice a year. An exception were the four years in the {{ill|Sothic cycle|en}}, when the heliacal rising fell on the first day of the first month of the year. This rare event was not repeated until about 1,452 years later. [[en:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] === Old and Middle Kingdom === In the Old and Middle Kingdoms, the first days of the month were called “openers.” In [[Festkalender des Niuserre|the festival calendar of Niuserre]], which was part of the decorations in the {{ill|Solar Temple of Niuserre|lt=Solar Temple of Niuserre|fr|Temple_solaire_d%27Abou_Ghorab|de|Sonnenheiligtum_des_Niuserre}}, the New Year celebration is linked to the first month of the season of Akhet. Another [[synonym]] attested is the month name “Month of the Re-Festival”. From the [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|12th]] The {{ill|8=de|9=Festkalender_der_Akrobatentruppe|festival calendar of the acrobat troupe}} dates back to the 1st [[Middle Kingdom of Egypt|dynasty]], in which the New Year's festival also fell on the first month of Akhet. === New Kingdom and Late Period === With the beginning of the [[New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom,]] the term “Opener” changed to “First Day”. The only term that remained was the naming of New Year’s Day as “Opener of the Year”. {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Alan Gardiner|de}} as well as {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} suspect that the “month of the Re-Festival” changed its annual form in the course of calendar history, which is why from the New Kingdom onwards the New Year festival was shifted to the twelfth month under the new name {{ill|Mesori|en}} . == See also == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hourly calendar of Ramses II|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|en}} == literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Richard-Anthony Parker|de}} : ''The calendars of ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Volume 26, {{ISSN|0081-7554}} ). University of Chicago Press, Chicago IL 1950. * Paule Posener-Kriéger: ''Msjw-Ra.'' In: ''Revue d'Egyptologie.'' ''(RdE).'' Volume 22, 1970, {{ISSN|0035-1849}}, p. 137. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|en}} : ''Ancient Egyptian festival dates'' (= ''Academy of Sciences and Literature. Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Volume 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}} ).
